_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Plain-language summary:** CSRD requires companies above defined size thresholds to publish audited sustainability reports — covering environmental, social, and governance topics — using the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). Reports must disclose material risks, impacts, and sustainability performance, including Scope 3 value-chain emissions. The first in-scope companies (Wave 1: large listed companies, large EU companies previously subject to NFRD) were required to report for financial year 2024, with reports due in 2025.

**Why it applies to the workspace — two distinct pathways:**

**Pathway 1 — Direct obligation (size-threshold dependent):** If the workspace meets the applicable employee and financial thresholds, it falls directly within CSRD scope and must produce its own compliant report. *Legal Confirmation Required: whether the workspace meets current or revised thresholds is a factual determination requiring legal counsel review against the workspace's consolidated group structure.*

**Pathway 2 — Indirect pull-through (operative regardless of direct scope):** Freight forwarders are embedded in the value chains of large manufacturers, retailers, luxury houses, event producers, film studios, and automotive OEMs — many of which are Wave 1 or Wave 2 CSRD reporters. CSRD's ESRS require reporters to disclose material Scope 3 emissions, which under the GHG Protocol include Category 4 (upstream transportation and distribution) and Category 9 (downstream transportation and distribution). This means the workspace's largest clients — across live events, luxury goods, fine art, film and TV, and high-value automotive — will require shipment-level emissions data from the workspace to populate their own mandatory disclosures.

The Omnibus I proposal raises the direct-scope threshold to companies with more than 1,000 employees; the stop-the-clock Directive postpones Wave 2 and Wave 3 reporting requirements. However, neither measure removes the indirect pull-through obligation: Wave 1 companies are already reporting, and their demand for supplier emissions data is already active.

*Source: European Commission — Corporate Sustainability Reporting, DG FISMA, last updated 9 December 2025. https://finance.ec.europa.eu/capital-markets-union-and-financial-markets/company-reporting-and-auditing/company-reporting/corporate-sustainability-reporting_en*

*Source: EUR-Lex — Text of CSRD (2022/2464/EU), EUR-Lex/Official Journal of the European Union. https://eur-lex.europa.eu*

---
